title: REST API docker-compat, gitlab-runner
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

gitlab-runner fails to run tasks using the docker compatible API

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. install gitlab-runner for your distro

2. register gitlab-runner so it can run the jobs (gitlab-runner register -n -u "https://gitlab.com/" -r "<project key>" --name "builderpodman" --executor "docker" --docker-image "<base image>" --tag-list "podmanrunner" --run-untagged=false)

3. Create a job for the job in gl (in my case I created one for docker and one for podman, to compare)
```yml
stages:
  - build

.build:containertest:
  stage: build
  before_script:
    - echo "${EXECUTOR} running before script"
  script:
    - echo "${EXECUTOR} running script"

build:docker:
  extends: .build:containertest
  tags:
    - dockerrunner
  variables:
    EXECUTOR: "docker"
    CI_DEBUG_TRACE: "true"

build:podman:
  extends: .build:containertest
  tags:
    - podmanrunner
  variables:
    EXECUTOR: "podman"
    CI_DEBUG_TRACE: "true"
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```Running with gitlab-runner 13.3.1 (738bbe5a)
  on builderpodman ZAq3tJzX
Preparing the "docker" executor
00:39
Using Docker executor with image arm64v8/centos:8 ...
Pulling docker image gitlab/gitlab-runner-helper:arm64-738bbe5a ...
Using docker image sha256:98583b9e0b760274a726eb36f90a47e5bf4ac0750fa7fad8b907b994a7e98479 for gitlab/gitlab-runner-helper:arm64-738bbe5a ...
Pulling docker image arm64v8/centos:8 ...
Using docker image sha256:9d5bccb1776bd298334234395ecd1e33ec7050e8ee3fc821daed7c484588b17b for arm64v8/centos:8 ...
Preparing environment
Getting source from Git repository
Executing "step_script" stage of the job script
Job succeeded
```
Job succeeds but nothing is actually being executed https://gitlab.com/psgreco/podman_tests/-/jobs/745351978

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
Running with gitlab-runner 13.2.2 (a998cacd)
  on builderdocker 5JcQ-XCB
Preparing the "docker" executor
00:06
Using Docker executor with image arm64v8/centos:8 ...
Pulling docker image arm64v8/centos:8 ...
Using docker image sha256:9d5bccb1776bd298334234395ecd1e33ec7050e8ee3fc821daed7c484588b17b for arm64v8/centos:8 ...
Preparing environment
Running on runner-5jcq-xcb-project-21214845-concurrent-0 via c7-docker-runner...
Getting source from Git repository
Fetching changes with git depth set to 50...
Initialized empty Git repository in /builds/psgreco/podman_tests/.git/
Created fresh repository.
Checking out e1797bc6 as tests...
Skipping Git submodules setup
Executing "step_script" stage of the job script
$ echo "${EXECUTOR} running before script"
docker running before script
$ echo "${EXECUTOR} running script"
docker running script
Job succeeded
``` 
Job is normally executed (example from docker) https://gitlab.com/psgreco/podman_tests/-/jobs/745321893



**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:      2.1.0-rc1
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Mon Sep 14 17:22:18 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
[podman_system_service.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5246295/podman_system_service.txt)
[gitlab_runner_job1.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5246341/gitlab_runner_job1.txt)

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
# rpm -q podman
podman-2.1.0-0.2.rc1.fc32.aarch64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running on ARM64
Logs may not belong to the same execution, since tests were ran several times

comments.body: @psgreco any chance we could talk on IRC about this one ?

> @psgreco any chance we could talk on IRC about this one ?

Sure, ping me on #podman or PM (pgreco on irc)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@psgreco @baude What is the skoop on this issue?  Can we close it?
I don't have any problems remaining, I'm ok with closing
@psgreco what was the resolution to this issue?

I see that @psgreco subsequently authored this article: [The history of an API: GitLab Runner and Podman
](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/history-api)
@jchristi I've been using the API without issues since that article was written, it is still not enough for for gitlab people to declare it ready, but for me i is more than "good enough"
